item.name: Retrieve tracking information of a parcel

request.name: Retrieve tracking information of a parcel

description.content: Fetches detailed tracking information, including the status history of the parcel.

variable.description: (Required) The tracking number that the user received

response.name: OK

variable.description: (Required) The tracking number that the user received

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.body:

response.name:
Not Found.

 When the parcel cannot be found, the response is returned as an XML document from AWS S3.

Detailed information about the error structure can be found in the S3 documentation:

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html#RESTErrorResponses

variable.description: (Required) The tracking number that the user received

response.name: Requests to our API are limited on a per Sendcloud account basis. If you have multiple API keys per Sendcloud account, the budget is shared between these keys. If you get rate limited, we return an empty response with HTTP status code 429.

variable.description: (Required) The tracking number that the user received

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

The Tracking API allows you to obtain the status of a given parcel at any point during the delivery journey, across all Sendcloud supported carriers.

Contact Support:
 Name: Sendcloud API Support
